Military


Ukraine Conflict News





16 December 2025 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • Venezuela protests to UN Security Council over US seizure of oil tanker Press TV 16 Dec 2025 -- Venezuela has protested to the United Nations Security Council over the United States recent seizure of one of its oil tankers.
  • US boat strikes kill 8 in eastern Pacific as Washington escalates pressure on Venezuela Press TV 16 Dec 2025 -- The US military says it has carried out new lethal strikes on boats in the eastern Pacific Ocean, killing eight people, as Washington intensifies its military assaults against what it claims to be drug trafficking, but which Caracas says is designed to destabilize Venezuela.
  • Venezuela dismisses EU sanctions renewal as 'futile,' blasts Europe's lack of political autonomy Press TV 16 Dec 2025 -- Venezuela has condemned a decision by the European Council to renew sanctions against the country until 2027, describing the measures as "futile," a "complete failure," and a sign of the EU's lack of autonomy in global affairs.
  • US Military Strikes 3 Alleged Drug-Running Ships, Killing 8 People Sputnik 16 Dec 2025 -- The US Southern Command announced on Tuesday that it had struck three vessels allegedly carrying drugs in international waters in the Pacific Ocean, killing eight people on board.
  • UN rights chief warns of deepening repression in Venezuela, rising toll in Ukraine UN News 16 Dec 2025 -- The UN's top human rights official warned on Tuesday that repression is tightening its grip in Venezuela while civilians in Ukraine face an ever-rising toll, as global attention drifts from two crises marked by deepening abuses and shrinking accountability.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • War Department Reevaluates Discharge Characterizations for COVID Vaccine Refusal DoD News 16 Dec 2025 -- For service members involuntarily discharged solely for refusing to take the COVID-19 vaccine, the War Department has ordered a reevaluation of their discharge status to see who might be eligible for an upgrade to an honorable discharge.
  • Top ASC leaders conduct APS-2 site visits, key leader engagements across Europe Army News 16 Dec 2025 -- The command team and other senior leaders from U.S. Army Sustainment Command visited multiple Army Prepositioned Stocks-2 worksites across Europe and met with senior leaders from the 405th Army Field Support Brigade, 21st Theater Sustainment Command, and other key units and organizations, Dec. 9-12, 2025.
  • 332nd AEW rolls towards modernization with new vehicle fleet CENTCOM 16 Dec 2025 -- The 332nd Expeditionary Logistics Readiness Squadron received new Joint Light Tactical Vehicles, improving the fleet used for base defense and increasing safety and survivability in challenging terrain, in the U.S. Central Command area of responsibility, Dec. 3, 2025.
  • U.S. Navy Accepts Delivery of USNS Lucy Stone US Navy 16 Dec 2025 -- John Lewis-class fleet replenishment oiler USNS Lucy Stone (T-AO 209) was delivered to the Navy, Dec. 16. The delivery marks the official transfer of the ship from shipbuilder General Dynamics NASSCO to the Navy.
  • U.S. Navy, ASEAN members conclude AUMX 20 US Navy 16 Dec 2025 -- U.S. Navy Sailors from Independence-variant littoral combat ship USS Cincinnati (LCS 20) and Commander, Destroyer Squadron (DESRON) 7, rejoined service members from the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) ashore in Batam, after concluding the ASEAN-U.S. Maritime Exercise (AUMX) sea phase in the Singapore Strait, Dec. 13.

  • "This is how we ensure allied forces remain prepared and capable of prevailing against any potential threat." NATO Exercise LOYAL DOLOS 2025 - a powerful demonstration of allied cohesion and interoperability NATO Allied Land Command 16 Dec 2025 -- Approximately 1,500 soldiers and civilians across multiple European locations worked side by side to assess the NATO Rapid Deployable Corps - Greece and its capabilities as a NATO Warfighting Corps.

  • Russia Plans Laying of Two Project 677 Submarines in Early 2026 - Navy Commander-in-Chief Sputnik 16 Dec 2025 -- Two more Project 677 diesel-electric submarines are scheduled to be laid in early 2026, Russian Navy Commander-in-Chief Adm. Aleksandr Moiseyev said on Tuesday.

  • 32nd meeting of the Ukraine Defense Contact Group (UDCG) Poland - Ministry of National Defence 16 Dec 2025 -- The 32nd meeting of the Ukraine Defense Contact Group (UDCG), in which the secretary of state in the ministry of defence Paweł Zalewski participated, was held on 16 December.
  • $45 billion in support: Denys Shmyhal says 2025 has been the most productive year for Ukraine, speaking at the Ramstein meeting Ukraine MOD 16 Dec 2025
  • MoD supports the training of specialists under a new quality assurance programme for the defense industry Ukraine MOD 16 Dec 2025
  • US offers Ukraine 'strong' security guarantees amid unsettled territorial row Press TV 16 Dec 2025 -- The United States has offered to provide Ukraine with NATO-style security guarantees as American and European negotiators report progress in talks to end the conflict in the former Soviet republic, despite Kiev's unsettled row over territorial concessions.
  • US Urges Ukraine to Accept Last Offer of 'Platinum' Security Guarantees - Reports Sputnik 16 Dec 2025 -- The United States has told Ukraine to accept its "platinum standard" offer of security guarantees or lose it, The Telegraph newspaper reported, citing US officials.

  • Readout of December 2025 U.S.-China Defense Policy Coordination Talks DoD 16 Dec 2025
  • China-Singapore joint army training carries out field drills on urban counter-terrorism PRC MND 16 Dec 2025 -- Participants from both sides of the China-Singapore joint army training recently conducted field drills focused on urban counter-terrorism operations.

  • Three of a kind in Malaysia Australia DOD 16 Dec 2025 -- Operation Gateway's latest rotation in November and December 2025 witnessed a trio of RAAF P-8A Poseidons operating from Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF) Base Butterworth.

  • Japan - Aegis Class Destroyer Support DSCA 16 Dec 2025 -- The State Department has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of Japan for Aegis Class Destroyer Support and related equipment for an estimated cost of $100.2 million. The Defense Security Cooperation Agency delivered the required certification notifying Congress.
  • Japan-UAE Military to Military (MM) Dialogue Ministry of Defense of Japan 16 Dec 2025
  • Designation of defense-related facilities based on the Drone Act Ministry of Defense of Japan 16 Dec 2025 -- Based on the Article 6-1, 6-2 and 6-4 of the Drone Act (Act No. 9 of 2016), the Minister of Defense has issued a public notice designating the defense-related facilities, sites or areas of the defense-related facilities, and the areas surrounding the defense-related facilities.

  • COMMISSIONING OF DSC A20 (FIRST DIVING SUPPORT CRAFT) India PIB 16 Dec 2025 -- The Indian Navy commissioned DSC A20, first of the five Diving Support Craft (DSC), built indigenously by M/s Titagarh Rail System Limited, during a ceremony held at Naval Base, Kochi, on 16 Dec 2025

  • Lebanon - M1151A1 High Mobility Multi-Purpose Wheeled Vehicles DSCA 16 Dec 2025 -- The State Department has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of Lebanon of M1151A1 High Mobility Multi-Purpose Wheeled Vehicles and related equipment for an estimated cost of $34.5 million. The Defense Security Cooperation Agency delivered the required certification notifying Congress.

  • Eight EU leaders in Helsinki: All of Europe is being defended on the eastern flank Finnish Government 16 Dec 2025 -- Eight EU Member States stressed the importance of significantly strengthening security at Europe's eastern external border at the Eastern Flank Summit in Helsinki on 16 December.

  • The President in Helsinki: Europe's security is defended on the Eastern flank Lithuania MFA 16 Dec 2025 -- On Tuesday in Helsinki, President Gitanas Nausėda took part in the Eastern Flank Summit, convened by Finnish Prime Minister Petteri Orpo.

  • Enhanced bilateral cooperation with the USA Romania - Ministry of National Defence 16 Dec 2025 -- On Monday, December 15th, State Secretary for Defence Policy, Planning and International Relations, Sorin-Dan Moldovan, received at the Ministry of Defence headquarters, the visit of a delegation from the US Department of State, led by Christopher W. Smith, Deputy Assistant Secretary, Bureau of European and Eurasian Affairs.

  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with DRC Foreign Minister Kayikwamba Wagner US Dept. of State 16 Dec 2025
  • Secretary of State Marco Rubio Remarks to the Press US Dept. of State 16 Dec 2025

  • Quad Concludes Field Training Exercise to Advance Indo-Pacific Logistics Network US Dept. of State 16 Dec 2025 -- From December 8 to 12, Quad partners (Australia, India, Japan and the United States) successfully conducted their first Field Training Exercise (FTX) on the sidelines of Operation Christmas Drop at Andersen Air Force Base, Guam as part of the Quad Indo-Pacific Logistics Network (IPLN).
  • Field Training Exercise of the Quad Indo-Pacific Logistics Network in Guam Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 16 Dec 2025

Defense Industry

  • Contracts For Dec. 16, 2025

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 16 December 2025 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 16 Dec 2025

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Statement by PM Netanyahu Israel - Prime Minister 16 Dec 2025

  • Khaled Meshaal: Trump should listen to MAGA base and abandon Israel-first policy Press TV 16 Dec 2025 -- Senior Hamas leader Khaled Meshaal has urged US President Donald Trump to abandon Washington's Israel-first policy, saying even Trump's own MAGA supporters now view Israel as a "burden" that harms US interests.

  • Gaza ceasefire still fragile as cold turns deadly, Security Council hears UN News 16 Dec 2025 -- The ceasefire in Gaza remains fragile, while humanitarians continue to face obstructions in delivering aid to the people, a senior UN official working to promote greater peace in the region told the Security Council on Tuesday.
  • New demolition order for West Bank camp is 'more devastating news' UN News 16 Dec 2025 -- Israel has issued new demolition orders for the Nur Shams camp in the north of the occupied West Bank effective this week, which a senior official with the UN Palestine refugee agency (UNRWA) labelled as "more devastating news" for the region.
  • UN General Assembly reaffirms Palestinians' right to self-determination IRNA 16 Dec 2025 -- The United Nations General Assembly has passed a resolution reaffirming the Palestinian people's right to self-determination, including their right to establish an independent State of Palestine.
  • Lebanon: UNRWA Protection Update (October - December 2024) UNRWA 16 Dec 2025
  • Lebanon: UNRWA Protection Update (January - March 2024) UNRWA 16 Dec 2025
  • Gaza Ceasefire Still Precarious, Deputy Special Coordinator Tells Security Council, Citing Staggering Humanitarian Needs UN Security Council 16 Dec 2025 -- As the Security Council today held its final mandated meeting on the question of Palestine for 2025, its outgoing members urged action amidst flagging implementation of the peace plan that the organ endorsed in November.

  • White House issues warning to Netanyahu over Gaza ceasefire violation: Report Press TV 16 Dec 2025 -- The White House has delivered a stern message to Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu regarding his regime's recent aggressions in Gaza, which violate a ceasefire agreement brokered by US President Donald Trump.

  • Israel must lift all barriers to aid without delay and let international NGOs operate freely: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 16 Dec 2025 -- Statement by Ambassador James Kariuki, UK Chargé d'Affaires to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on Palestine.
  • We strongly condemn the unprecedented rise in settler violence against Palestinian civilians in the West Bank and East Jerusalem: E5 statement on the West Bank and East Jerusalem UK FCDO 16 Dec 2025 -- E5 statement delivered by Ambassador James Kariuki, UK Chargé d'Affaires to the UN, on behalf of Denmark, France, Greece, Slovenia and the United Kingdom.
  • Joint Statement Issued by the Consultative Meeting on Developments Relating to the Cause of Palestine with the Participation of High-Level Delegations from the General Secretariats of the OIC, the League of Arab States, and the AU Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 16 Dec 2025
  • Gazans are Dying from the Cold, Under the Rubble of Crumbling Buildings and Israeli Bombardment -----Settler Attacks Have Been Increasing by 36% Annually in the West Bank Since 2020 Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 16 Dec 2025 -- The OIC Media Observatory on Israeli Crimes Against Palestinians recorded 78 settler attacks on Palestinian towns and villages between December 9 and 15, 2025.
  • Turkey excluded from post-war Gaza conference in Doha due to Israeli veto: Report Press TV 16 Dec 2025 -- Turkey has reportedly been excluded from a US-led military conference on post-war Gaza in Qatar, a move widely attributed to Israeli opposition, despite Ankara's declared readiness to participate and its strong ties with Washington and Doha.
  • Iran Foreign Ministry: Israel's backers complicit in Gaza genocide Press TV 16 Dec 2025 -- Iran has strongly condemned the continuation of the Israeli genocide in Gaza through the regime's incessant bombardments and the blockade of humanitarian aid entry into the war-torn territory, saying Israel's backers are complicit in the genocide.
  • Foreign Ministry Expresses the Kingdom's Condemnation of the Israeli Occupation Authorities' Approval of 19 Settlements in the Occupied West Bank SPA 16 Dec 2025 -- The Ministry of Foreign Affairs expresses the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's condemnation of the Israeli occupation authorities' approval of the construction of 19 settlements in the occupied West Bank, in violation of relevant UN resolutions
  • US Gaza Plan Prevented Bloodshed, Yet Lasting Ceasefire Not Achieved - Russian Envoy to UN Sputnik 16 Dec 2025 -- US President Donald Trump's peace plan has helped reduce large-scale fighting and hunger in the Gaza Strip, though a lasting ceasefire remains elusive, Russian Deputy Permanent Representative to the United Nations Dmitry Polyanskiy said on Tuesday.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Occupied Palestinian Territory, Democratic Republic of the Congo UNOCHA 16 Dec 2025

  • PH, US forces hold 10th bilateral exercise in WPS Philippine News Agency 16 Dec 2025 -- Naval and air units from the Philippines and the United States held the 10th bilateral maritime cooperative activity (MCA) in the West Philippine Sea (WPS), highlighting the country's growing capability to integrate with allied forces.
  • DND chief denounces China's 'dangerous, inhumane' actions in WPS Philippine News Agency 16 Dec 2025 -- Department of National Defense (DND) Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr. on Tuesday scored China for the "dangerous and inhumane actions" taken by its maritime forces against Filipino fisherfolk in Escoda Shoal (also known as Sabina Shoal) in the West Philippine Sea.

  • Latest food security report confirms fears of deepening hunger crisis in Afghanistan as winter sets in WFP 16 Dec 2025 -- Over 17 million Afghans are facing acute food insecurity this winter, as the scale and severity of hunger and malnutrition deepens, warns the United Nations World Food Programme (WFP). New food security figures from the latest Integrated Food Security Phase Classification (IPC) report for Afghanistan indicate that three million more women, men, and children face acute hunger or worse (IPC3+) compared to the 14.8 million last year.

  • Ranking Member Shaheen, Representative Wilson Pen Foreign Policy Op-Ed, "One Year on, U.S. Sanctions are Killing Syria's Recovery," Ahead of Senate Vote on Annual Defense Bill US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 16 Dec 2025 -- Today, U.S. Senator Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, and U.S. Representative Joe Wilson (R-SC) published an op-ed in Foreign Policy ahead of the Senate's vote on the Fiscal Year 2026 National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA), which includes a measure to repeal Caesar Syria Civilian Protection Act sanctions on Syria.
  • WFP expands and extends its Subsidized Bread Programme across Syria WFP 16 Dec 2025 -- The United Nations World Food Programme (WFP), in partnership with the Ministry of Economy and Industry, is expanding and extending its Subsidized Bread Programme through March 2026,thanks to a generous contribution from the United States government. Beginning in January 2026, distributions will support over 300 bakeries nationwide, some of which were previously rehabilitated by WFP, providing daily bread assistance to more than 4 million people.

  • Three policemen killed while confronting terrorists at checkpoint in southeast Iran Press TV 16 Dec 2025 -- At least three police officers have been shot dead during an armed confrontation with a group of unidentified terrorists at a security checkpoint in Iran's southeastern city of Kerman.

  • Sudan: Intensifying hostilities bring new displacement, more casualties UN News 16 Dec 2025 -- The situation across Sudan's Kordofan region is rapidly worsening, the UN aid coordination office (OCHA) warned on Monday.
  • Sudan: Intensifying hostilities in Kordofan OHCHR 16 Dec 2025

  • Security Council Sanctions Committee concerning South Sudan Discusses Interim Report of Panel of Experts UN Security Council 16 Dec 2025 -- On 11 November 2025, the Coordinator of the Panel of Experts on South Sudan briefed the members of the Security Council Committee established pursuant to resolution 2206 (2015), in connection with the Panel's interim report submitted in pursuance of paragraph 18 of resolution 2781 (2025).

News Reports

  • RESTRICTING AND LIMITING THE ENTRY OF FOREIGN NATIONALS TO PROTECT THE SECURITY OF THE UNITED STATES The White House 16 Dec 2025
  • Fact Sheet: President Donald J. Trump Further Restricts and Limits the Entry of Foreign Nationals to Protect the Security of the United States The White House 16 Dec 2025

  • Trump signs order declaring fentanyl "weapon of mass destruction" People's Daily 16 Dec 2025 -- U.S. President Donald Trump signed an executive order on Monday classifying fentanyl and its core precursor chemicals as a "weapon of mass destruction."

  • Second Meeting of the U.S.-Mexico Security Implementation Group US Dept. of State 16 Dec 2025 -- On December 11, representatives from six United States government agencies and their Mexican counterparts convened the second Security Implementation Group (SIG) meeting in Mexico City, advancing security cooperation as sovereign partners to improve the safety and welfare of citizens on both sides of the border.

  • Terrorist Designations of Clan del Golfo US Dept. of State 16 Dec 2025 -- Today, the Department of State is designating Clan del Golfo as a Foreign Terrorist Organization (FTO) and a Specially Designated Global Terrorist (SDGT).
  • 4 arrested for plotting New Year's Eve bombings in California: U.S. authorities People's Daily 16 Dec 2025 -- Four people accused of plotting New Year's Eve bombings in California have been arrested, U.S. authorities said Monday.

  • Dual national faces trial for spying for Israel's Mossad during 12-day war: Iran's judiciary Press TV 16 Dec 2025 -- The spokesman for the Iranian judiciary says a dual national has been arrested and put on trial on charges of affiliation with Israel's Mossad and collaboration with the espionage agency during the 12-day Israeli-US aggression against the Islamic Republic in June.

  • League condemns terrorist attack on Sydney, Australia League of Arab States 16 Dec 2025
  • UN expert condemns Bondi Beach terrorist attack on Jewish Australians OHCHR 16 Dec 2025 -- The Special Rapporteur on the Promotion and Protection of Human Rights and Fundamental Freedoms while Countering Terrorism, Ben Saul, today condemned the apparent terrorist attack that targeted Jewish people during a Hannukah celebration at Bondi Beach on 14 December 2025.
  • PH, Australia to exchange info on probe over Bondi Beach shooting Philippine News Agency 16 Dec 2025 -- The Philippines and Australia's top diplomats have agreed to "keep each other closely informed" of any developments related to the probe into the Bondi Beach shooting, said the Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) on Tuesday.
  • NSC probing Bondi Beach shooters' alleged PH travel - Palace Philippine News Agency 16 Dec 2025 -- The National Security Council (NSC) is currently looking into reports that the father and son behind Australia's deadliest mass shooting traveled to the Philippines a month before the attack.

  • EU Sanctions Envoy Says Measures Steadily And Increasingly Choking Russia Of Key Revenues RFE/RL 16 Dec 2025 -- European Union sanctions are "slowly but surely" choking Russia's oil exports - and are also stepping up in speed and scope, according to the 27-nation bloc's chief sanctions official.
  • Working meeting with Head of Kabardino-Balkaria Kazbek Kokov President of Russia 16 Dec 2025 -- Vladimir Putin held a working meeting with Head of the Kabardino-Balkarian Republic Kazbek Kokov in the Kremlin. Mr Kokov briefed the President on the socioeconomic situation in the region.
  • Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Dmitry Lyubinsky's remarks at the BRICS Special Event on Incentives for the Private Sector to counter corruption, Doha, December 16, 2025 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 16 Dec 2025
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's remarks at the 45th meeting of the Foreign Ministry's Council of Heads of Constituent Entities of the Russian Federation on stepping up partnerships in Africa, Moscow, December 16, 2025 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 16 Dec 2025
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's remarks at the 45th meeting of the Foreign Ministry's Council of Heads of Constituent Entities of the Russian Federation on the Arctic dimension of region-to-region ties, Moscow, December 16, 2025 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 16 Dec 2025
  • Director of the Fourth Department of the CIS countries Mikhail Kalugin's interview with Rossiya Segodnya Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 16 Dec 2025
  • Ranking Member Shaheen Speaks on Senate Floor in Support of Designating Russia a State Sponsor of Terrorism US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 16 Dec 2025 -- Today, U.S. Senator Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, spoke on the Senate floor in support of Senator Lindsey Graham's (R-SC) Designating the Russian Federation as a State Sponsor of Terrorism Act. The bill directs the Secretary of State to assess whether, based on Russia's deliberate targeting of civilians, systematic abductions of Ukrainian children and other acts, Russia should be designated a State Sponsor of Terrorism.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on December 16, 2025 PRC MofA 16 Dec 2025
  • Wang Yi Has a Phone Call with Egyptian Foreign Minister Badr Abdelatty PRC MofA 16 Dec 2025 -- On December 16, 2025, Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Foreign Minister Wang Yi had a phone call with Egyptian Foreign Minister Badr Abdelatty at the latter's request.
  • Chinese FM slams Japanese defense minister's claims on China's military spending as 'groundless, egregious and despicable' Global Times 16 Dec 2025 -- Japan's groundless accusations and malicious smears against China's legitimate national defense development are egregious and despicable, Chinese Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Tuesday in response to Japanese Defense Minister Shinjiro Koizumi's claims that China's military expenditure had grown sevenfold over two decades and its growth rate in the past three years had also significantly exceeded Japan's. Guo said that Japan, a nation with a severe record of aggression, has no right whatsoever to make irresponsible remarks.
  • HK's chief executive, Chinese embassies, central govt's authorities in HK reject Western smear on Jimmy Lai's verdict Global Times 16 Dec 2025 -- Hong Kong's chief executive, some overseas Chinese embassies, and the central government's authorities in the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region (HKSAR) refuted comments by some Western countries and media that have distorted the judiciary proceedings of Jimmy Lai.
  • Xi hears report from HKSAR chief executive PRC MND 16 Dec 2025 -- President Xi Jinping on Tuesday met with Chief Executive of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region (HKSAR) John Lee, who is on a duty visit to Beijing.
  • Xi hears report from Macao SAR chief executive PRC MND 16 Dec 2025 -- President Xi Jinping on Tuesday met with Chief Executive of the Macao Special Administrative Region (SAR) Sam Hou Fai, who is on a duty visit to Beijing.
  • HKSAR govt expresses strong dissatisfaction, opposition to US, Western countries, anti-China groups for malicious attacks following verdict of Jimmy Lai Global Times 16 Dec 2025 -- The Hong Kong Special Administrative Region (HKSAR) government expressed late Monday evening strong dissatisfaction with and opposition to the US and Western countries, anti-China media, organizations and politicians for their malicious attacks, false statements, and smears against the HKSAR, following the Hong Kong High Court's conviction judgment in Jimmy Lai's case, which was made strictly in accordance with the law and evidence.
  • Hong Kong court finds Jimmy Lai guilty of violating national security law based on hard evidence People's Daily 16 Dec 2025 -- Jimmy Lai, an instigator of anti-China riots in Hong Kong, was found guilty on Monday on two charges of conspiring to collude with external forces and a charge of conspiracy to publish seditious materials.
  • Verdict on Jimmy Lai is reached strictly in accordance with law and evidence: HKSAR gov't People's Daily 16 Dec 2025 -- The verdict on the national security case of Jimmy Lai is well-grounded and appropriate and fully demonstrates that the court has acted strictly in accordance with the law and evidence, a spokesperson with China's Hong Kong Special Administrative Region (HKSAR) government said on Monday.
  • Chinese state security authority warns foreign intelligence agencies using online games and anime to erode youth values Global Times 16 Dec 2025 -- China's Ministry of State Security (MSS) warned on Tuesday that foreign espionage and intelligence agencies, along with actors harboring ulterior motives, are increasingly exploiting online games, anime communities and video platforms popular among young people to spread historical nihilism and subversive narratives, aiming to subtly erode young people's sense of national identity and cultural confidence, posing risks to national political and cultural security.
  • China launches new satellite People's Daily 16 Dec 2025 -- China sent a new satellite into space on Tuesday from the Taiyuan Satellite Launch Center in the northern province of Shanxi.

  • Taiwan regional authorities' abusive use of 'dollar diplomacy' can't deceive public opinion: FM on DPP's claim regarding Honduras Global Times 16 Dec 2025 -- The Taiwan regional authorities' abusive use of "dollar diplomacy" under the guise of "trust" and "prosperity" cannot conceal the true nature of their unscrupulous pursuit of "Taiwan independence," nor can it deceive public opinion or the residents of the island, Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Tuesday, in response to reports that when asked how Taiwan could maintain its so-called "friendship" with Honduras while "competing" with the Chinese mainland, Lin Chia-lung, head of Taiwan's so-called foreign affairs authorities, claimed that Taiwan builds relationships based on trust and prosperity.
  • Japan still squeezes out toothpaste and plants spikes on key issues: FM responds to Japanese politician's latest claims on Taiwan question Global Times 16 Dec 2025 -- "From the recent series of statements made by the Japanese side, it is still squeezing out toothpaste and planting spikes on key issues, attempting to confuse the public and muddle through. China firmly opposes this, Chinses Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Tuesday, in response to media inquiry over Japanese politician's latest claims regarding Taiwan question.

  • Ruling on ex-S. Korean president Yoon's obstruction of arrest case to be delivered on Jan. 16 People's Daily 16 Dec 2025 -- A court handling the case against former impeached South Korean President Yoon Suk-yeol on charges including obstruction of arrest and deletion of encrypted phone records announced plans to deliver its ruling on Jan. 16 next year, multiple local media reported Tuesday.
  • Spokesperson's Press Briefing (December 16, 2025) ROK MFA 16 Dec 2025

  • Iran urges transparent and inclusive process in selection of future UN chief IRNA 16 Dec 2025 -- Iran's Ambassador and Permanent Representative to the UN Amir Saeid Iravani has called for a transparent and inclusive process in the selection of the next UN Secretary-General.
  • Iran urges fair process in selecting next UN chief, dismisses Rafael Grossi as unfit Press TV 16 Dec 2025 -- Iran has called for a fair process to select the next UN secretary-general, warning that candidates who remained silent on unlawful military attacks against Iran's peaceful nuclear program are unfit — in an apparent reference to the head of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA), Rafael Grossi.
  • Administration spokesperson: US bombed negotiation table IRNA 16 Dec 2025 -- Administration spokesperson Fatemeh Mohajerani says the whole world witnessed that it was the US that bombed the negotiation table.
  • Press release on the upcoming talks between Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 16 Dec 2025

  • PM Netanyahu at the Mossad Excellence Awards Ceremony at the President's Residence Israel - Prime Minister 16 Dec 2025
  • PM Netanyahu at the Hanukkah Candle Lighting with PMO Employees Israel - Prime Minister 16 Dec 2025
  • Prime Minister Netanyahu Lights the Second Hanukkah Candle with IDF Soldiers at the Western Wall Israel - Prime Minister 16 Dec 2025

  • Transcript of Special Briefing by MEA on Prime Minister's Visit to Jordan (December 16, 2025) India MEA 16 Dec 2025

  • Pakistan, Russia Discussing Oil Agreement - Finance Minister Sputnik 16 Dec 2025 -- The Russian and Pakistani energy ministries are discussing an oil agreement, and Pakistan would like to see it signed, Pakistani Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb said in an interview with Sputnik.

  • Chile: Statement by the Spokesperson on the outcome of the presidential elections European External Action Service (EEAS) 16 Dec 2025 -- The European Union congratulates José Antonio Kast upon his election as President of Chile, and the Chilean people for their participation in a peaceful and orderly electoral process. We look forward to working with President Kast and his administration, and remain committed to deepening our relationship in all areas of mutual interest.

  • Ministerial Committee on EU Affairs discusses European Council and Ukraine reparations loan Finnish Government 16 Dec 2025 -- In its meeting on Tuesday 16 December, the Ministerial Committee on European Union Affairs outlined Finland's positions for the European Council and the EU-Western Balkans summit taking place this week.

  • Finland participates in the establishment of Claims Commission ensuring accountability in Russia's war of aggression Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Finland 16 Dec 2025 -- Political State Secretary Pasi Rajala will lead Finland's delegation at the diplomatic conference in The Hague on 16 December 2025 for the adoption of the Convention Establishing an International Claims Commission for Ukraine.

  • Kushner-Linked Firm Reportedly Drops Belgrade Project Amid Controversy Over Site RFE/RL 16 Dec 2025 -- An investment company founded by Jared Kushner, the son-in-law of US President Donald Trump, has withdrawn from a planned $500 million hotel construction project in Belgrade amid a swirling controversy over the site, which was destroyed during the NATO bombing in 1999.

  • Wider Europe Briefing: Ukraine's Claims Commission And An EU-Balkans Summit RFE/RL 16 Dec 2025

  • World News in Brief: Landmark disease and mental health declaration, Afghan hunger deepens, DR Congo refugee crisis UN News 16 Dec 2025 -- World leaders meeting at the UN General Assembly have adopted a historic global declaration to tackle noncommunicable diseases and mental health conditions together, the World Health Organization (WHO) announced on Tuesday.
